description: HP 436A Power Meter with HP-IB (Option 022). Legacy instrument using single-letter ASCII program codes to set range, measurement mode, cal factor enable/disable, and measurement rate (triggering). Has no *IDN? or SCPI support; does not respond to serial poll, parallel poll, GTL, or Group Execute Trigger. Remote trigger capability is part of the data message (measurement rate code). Reading the instrument returns a fixed 14-character output data string (status + range + mode + measured value + exponent + CR LF). The order of program codes matters: the code for measurement rate should be sent last.

commands.name: rangeMostSensitive
commands.description: Select range 1 (most sensitive range). Note: range 1 requires up to ~10 seconds to settle.

commands.name: rangeAuto
commands.description: Select autorange. The Power Meter automatically selects the appropriate range.

commands.name: modeDbRel
commands.description: Select dB (Rel) mode; reading is relative to a stored dB [Ref] reference.

commands.name: modeDbRef
commands.description: Select dB [Ref] mode; stores the present RF input level as the reference for dB (Rel) measurements.

commands.name: sensorAutoZero
commands.description: Sensor auto-zero. Remove RF power from the sensor (or at least 20 dB below the lowest range) before zeroing. Typically programmed with a trigger and read back; check status character for auto-zero loop completion.

commands.name: calFactorDisable
commands.description: Disable cal factor; the Power Meter uses a cal factor of 100%.

commands.name: calFactorEnable
commands.description: Enable cal factor; the Power Meter uses the cal factor set by the front-panel CAL FACTOR % switch (the switch value itself cannot be set remotely).

commands.name: rateHold
commands.description: Hold. Inhibits the Power Meter from taking measurements and from outputting data; sets a reference condition from which a measurement can be triggered synchronously.

commands.name: triggerWithSettling
commands.description: Trigger with settling time (Trigger with Delay). Makes one measurement after executing a settling-time delay subroutine, outputs data, then goes to Hold until the next trigger.

commands.name: triggerImmediate
commands.description: Trigger immediate. Makes one measurement and outputs data in the minimum possible time (no settling-time delay), then goes to Hold until the next trigger.

commands.name: freeRunMaxRate
commands.description: Free run at maximum rate. Continuously takes measurements and outputs data in the minimum possible time, with no settling-time delay before each measurement.

commands.name: freeRunWithSettling
commands.description: Free run with settling time. Continuously takes measurements, executing a settling-time delay subroutine prior to each measurement.
